name: Almenara Castle and Cerro de la Cruz Circular Route
name_original: Castillo de Almenara y Pico Cruz — circular desde Puebla de Almenara
description: Hike a moderate 7.1-mile (11.4 km) circular route from Puebla de Almenara to explore Almenara Castle and enjoy panoramic views.
region_intro: Hiking around Mancha Alta offers diverse landscapes, encompassing mountainous and rugged terrains within the broader Castilla-La Mancha region. The area features natural parks with extensive networks of canyons, gorges, and river terraces carved by the Tajo River and its tributaries. Hikers can explore varied terrain, from plains with cereal fields and olive groves to significant mountain ranges like Sierra de Cuenca and Sierra de Alcaraz. This region provides a mix of geological formations, including unique rock formations and river valleys. Best hiking trails around Mancha Alta The most popular hiking route is Mirror of Valverde Trail (PR-CU 70), a 3.5 miles (5.6 km) trail that takes 1 hour 29 minutes to complete. This easy route offers a gentle path through rural landscapes. Another top favourite among local hikers is Las Cuerdas-Castillo de Garcimuñoz Trail, a moderate 5.9 miles (9.4 km) path. This trail features varied terrain leading towards the historic Castillo de Garcimuñoz. Local hikers also love the Trail of the Enchanted Moor (PR-CU 119), a 5.9 miles (9.5 km) trail leading through natural landscapes, often completed in about 2 hours 43 minutes. Hiking around Mancha Alta is defined by canyons and gorges, mountain ranges, and plains with olive groves. The network of hiking trails offers options for various ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging ascents. The routes in Mancha Alta are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.1 stars from over 40 reviews. More than 150 hikers have used komoot to explore Mancha Alta's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Mancha Alta?
A: Mancha Alta offers a wide variety of hiking experiences, with over 50 trails recorded on komoot. These routes cater to different skill levels, from easy walks to more challenging ascents.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when hiking in Mancha Alta?
A: The region boasts diverse terrain, ranging from plains with cereal fields, olive groves, and vineyards to significant mountain ranges like Sierra de Cuenca and Sierra de Alcaraz. You'll encounter natural parks featuring extensive networks of canyons, gorges, and river terraces, as well as unique geological formations and river valleys.

Q: Are there hiking options suitable for beginners or families with children?
A: Yes, Mancha Alta has numerous easy trails perfect for beginners or families. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1013187620/mirror-of-valverde-trail-pr-cu-70" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirror of Valverde Trail (PR-CU 70)&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 3.5-mile route offering a gentle path through rural landscapes. Overall, there are 26 easy routes available in the region.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Mancha Alta?
A: The hiking routes in Mancha Alta are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.1 stars from over 40 reviews. Hikers often praise the varied terrain, the historical sites, and the natural beauty of the canyons and gorges.

Q: Are there any historical sites or landmarks to see along the hiking trails?
A: Absolutely. Mancha Alta is rich in history. You can explore routes that pass by medieval castles, such as the historic Castillo de Garcimuñoz, which is featured on the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1013207276/las-cuerdas-castillo-de-garcimunoz-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Cuerdas-Castillo de Garcimuñoz Trail&lt;/a&gt;. Another notable historical site is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5399123" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Segóbriga Archaeological Park&lt;/a&gt;, which has a dedicated loop trail, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1012862411/segobriga-archaeological-park-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Segóbriga Archaeological Park Loop&lt;/a&gt;. You might also encounter iconic windmills, famously associated with Don Quixote, in high areas offering panoramic views.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in Mancha Alta?
A: Yes, there are several circular routes available. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1146919415/windmill-and-tajo-segura-transfer-circular-from-zafra-de-zancara" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Windmill and Tajo-Segura transfer — circular from Zafra de Záncara&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a scenic loop. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1154638139/almenara-castle-and-cerro-de-la-cruz-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almenara Castle and Cerro de la Cruz Circular Route&lt;/a&gt;, providing a great way to explore the area and its landmarks.

Q: Are there any trails that feature waterfalls or significant water bodies?
A: While specific waterfall trails are not highlighted in the immediate area, the region is characterized by numerous rivers like the Tajo, Guadiana, and Júcar, which carve out beautiful valleys and canyons. The Alto Tajo Natural Park, for instance, is known for its extensive network of canyons and gorges carved by the Tajo River and its tributaries. The nearby Los Calares del Río Mundo y de la Sima Natural Park is home to the spectacular 80-meter waterfall at the source of the Mundo river.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Mancha Alta?
A: Mancha Alta offers distinct beauty in all four seasons. Spring brings vibrant colors, while warm summers are ideal for enjoying natural water paradises like the Lagunas de Ruidera. Autumn provides pleasant temperatures and beautiful foliage. Even winter offers unique landscapes with occasional snowfall in the higher peaks of the Sierra Norte Natural Park.

Q: Are there any natural parks or protected areas to explore in Mancha Alta?
A: Yes, Mancha Alta encompasses several significant natural parks. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5050337" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto Tajo Natural Park&lt;/a&gt; is notable for its extensive canyons and gorges. The Sierra de Cuenca region includes the Serrania de Cuenca Natural Park, famous for its unique rock formations like the 'Enchanted City'. The Sierra Norte Natural Park in Guadalajara also offers diverse topography and rich biodiversity.

Q: Are there any trails that lead to viewpoints with panoramic views?
A: Many trails in Mancha Alta offer excellent viewpoints, especially those in the mountainous areas or near historical sites. The iconic windmills, such as those in Consuegra, are often situated in high areas and provide panoramic views of the surrounding plains. You can also find specific viewpoints like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4214036" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of the Castle of Puebla de Almenara&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What are some other interesting places to explore near the hiking trails?
A: Beyond the trails, you can visit charming settlements like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5636998" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Huete&lt;/a&gt;, or historical religious buildings such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5917394" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monastery of Uclés and Castle&lt;/a&gt;. The region also features unique geological formations and caves, including the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/8201244" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bache Cave&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6174510" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rock-Cut Necropolis with Carved Symbol&lt;/a&gt;.
tips:
Also known as Santiago de Jalameña, this fortification dates back to the 12th century and was built on top of an old Arab site.
It was rebuilt between the 14th and 15th centuries and is now in a dilapidated state.
The name comes from the word Almenara, from the Arabic "almanára" or "manārah" which means "place where there is light" or lighthouse. This name comes from the use that was given to this location to make signals to other fortresses.

faqs.question: Where can I park for the Castillo de Almenara y Pico Cruz trail?
faqs.answer: The trail starts and ends in Puebla de Almenara. You should be able to find parking within the village, close to the trailhead, as it's a common starting point for this circular route.

faqs.question: Is the Castillo de Almenara y Pico Cruz trail suitable for families or beginners?
faqs.answer: This trail is classified as moderate. While the climb to the castle can be steep, it's generally manageable, even for families with children. However, be aware that parts of the descent from the castle might be abrupt and somewhat slippery, so caution is advised.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the trail?
faqs.answer: You'll traverse mountainous landscapes characteristic of the Serranía de Cuenca, including rock formations, ravines, and river gorges. The path to the castle, known as the "Senda del Castell," borders a hill, and within the castle grounds, you'll encounter walled enclosures and towers.

faqs.question: What are the main sights and views along the route?
faqs.answer: The most prominent feature is the historic Almenara Castle. From the castle, you'll get extensive 360-degree panoramic views of the surrounding "Mancha conquense," including cultivated fields, holm oak groves, savin junipers, and pine forests. On clear days, you can see distant towns and other historical sites.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The region of Cuenca is known for its natural beauty. While specific recommendations aren't provided, generally spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures for hiking in Spain, avoiding the summer heat and potential winter chill.

faqs.question: Is the Castillo de Almenara y Pico Cruz trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: The trail itself is generally open for dogs, but it's always best to keep them on a leash, especially given the moderate difficulty, potential slippery sections, and the presence of wildlife in the area. Check local regulations for the castle grounds specifically.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for the trail or castle?
faqs.answer: There is no mention of permits or entrance fees required for hiking the trail itself. Access to the castle ruins is typically free, but it's always good to check for any updated local information before your visit.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or stay near the trailhead in Puebla de Almenara?
faqs.answer: Puebla de Almenara is a small village, and while specific establishments aren't detailed, it's a local hub. You can likely find basic services, including some restaurants or cafes, and potentially rural accommodation options within or very close to the village.

faqs.question: What is the history behind the Castillo de Almenara?
faqs.answer: The Almenara Castle was rebuilt between the 14th and 15th centuries on an earlier Andalusian fortification, taken by the Order of Santiago in 1177. Its name, "Almenara," means "place of light" or "lighthouse," indicating its historical role as a signaling point. It was strategically important and associated with figures like the Mendoza family. While partly in ruins, it retains much of its original structure.

faqs.question: Is public transport available to Puebla de Almenara?
faqs.answer: Information on direct public transport to Puebla de Almenara for hikers is limited. It's advisable to check local bus schedules or consider driving, as this offers the most flexibility for accessing the trailhead.

faqs.question: Are there any specific challenges or tips for hiking this trail?
faqs.answer: While the trail is moderate, be mindful that the descent from the castle can be abrupt and somewhat slippery. Wearing appropriate hiking footwear with good grip is recommended. Also, the castle offers 360-degree views, so consider bringing binoculars for distant towns and landscapes.
